title: Incident Timeline Formatter
description: Given a list of incident events with Unix timestamps and messages (tab-separated), sort them chronologically and format them as a human-readable timeline. Each output line should be in the format '[HH:MM:SS] message' where the time is in UTC, computed from the Unix timestamp.
constraints: Each input line is tab-separated: unix_timestamp\tmessage
Sort events by timestamp ascending before formatting
Time format is [HH:MM:SS] using UTC, relative to start of day (timestamp % 86400)
Return empty string for empty input
Output lines are joined with newlines

examples.input:
1700000120	Alert fired
1700000000	Incident started
1700000060	Pager notified
examples.output:
[00:00:00] Incident started
[00:01:00] Pager notified
[00:02:00] Alert fired
examples.explanation: Events are reordered by timestamp. The absolute timestamps map to 00:00:00, 00:01:00, and 00:02:00 UTC respectively (seconds within the day).

test_cases.description: Three out-of-order events are sorted and formatted
test_cases.input:
1700000120	Alert fired
1700000000	Incident started
1700000060	Pager notified
test_cases.expected_output:
[00:00:00] Incident started
[00:01:00] Pager notified
[00:02:00] Alert fired
